name: toc
description: Table of contents with scroll-spy highlighting of the active section

meta.name: Toc
meta.description: Table of contents with scroll-spy highlighting of the active section

props.name: items
props.type: { id: string; label: string; level?: number }[]

props.name: activeId
props.description: Controlled active item id; disables built-in scroll-spy when set

props.name: onActiveChange
props.description: Called with the id of the active section when it changes.

props.name: labels
props.description: Overrides for the component’s user-visible strings (i18n).

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

examples.title: Basic
examples.code: <Toc items={[{ id: 'intro', label: 'Introduction' }, { id: 'usage', label: 'Usage' }, { id: 'api', label: 'API', level: 3 }]} />

examples.title: Controlled active item
examples.code: <Toc activeId="usage" items={[{ id: 'intro', label: 'Introduction' }, { id: 'usage', label: 'Usage' }]} />
examples.description: Pass activeId to drive the highlight yourself; scroll-spy is disabled.

whenToUse: Long-form pages (docs, articles) that benefit from in-page jump links
Highlighting which section is currently in view as the reader scrolls
whenNotToUse: Top-level site navigation between pages — use NavigationMenu or SideNav
Showing hierarchy depth of the current page — use Breadcrumb

antiPatterns.bad: Using Toc as the primary site navigation between routes
antiPatterns.why: Toc links to sections within the current document, not to other pages

related.name: SideNav
related.reason: SideNav navigates between pages; Toc navigates within one page

related.name: Breadcrumb
related.reason: Breadcrumb shows ancestor hierarchy; Toc shows in-page sections

intent.a11yRationale: Rendered as a <nav> landmark with a label; entries are real anchor links and the active one is marked aria-current="location" so assistive tech announces the current section

content.tone: Short labels matching the section headings
content.notes: Labels should mirror the on-page heading text

flexibility.note: Control the highlight externally, or omit it for built-in scroll-spy
Colors, focus ring, and touch target must resolve to --cascivo-* tokens
